profile.name: Name
profile.title: Profile Settings
profile.telegramDescription: Connect your tududi account to a Telegram bot to add items to your inbox via Telegram messages.
profile.telegramTokenDescription: Create a bot with @BotFather on Telegram and paste the token here.
profile.telegramConnected: Your Telegram account is connected! Send messages to your bot to add items to your tududi inbox.
profile.telegramSetupFailed: Failed to set up Telegram bot.
profile.telegramInstructions: Go to https://t.me/{{botUsername}} and start chatting with your bot to connect it to your tududi account.
profile.pollingNote: Polling periodically checks for new messages from Telegram and adds them to your inbox.
profile.pollingDescription: Polling periodically checks for new messages from Telegram and adds them to your inbox.
profile.taskSummaryDescription: Receive regular summaries of your tasks via Telegram. This feature requires Telegram integration to be set up.
profile.summaryFrequencyDescription: Choose how often you want to receive task summaries
profile.frequencyHelp: Choose how often you want to receive task summaries
profile.taskIntelligenceDescription: Show popup alerts while typing task names that suggest improvements like "Make it more descriptive!", "Be more specific!", or "Add an action verb!". Disable this if you prefer typing in your own shorthand without suggestions.
profile.autoSuggestNextActionsDescription: When creating a project, automatically prompt for the very next physical action to take.
profile.pomodoroDescription: Enable the Pomodoro timer in the navigation bar for focused work sessions.
profile.kanbanDescription: Enable the Kanban Board for tracking task progress across swimlanes.
profile.productivityAssistantDescription: Show productivity insights that help identify stalled projects, vague tasks, and workflow improvements on your Today page.

shortcuts.enableDescription: Turn keyboard shortcuts on or off globally.
shortcuts.info: Keyboard shortcuts help you navigate quickly. Changes are saved when you click "Save Changes" at the bottom of the page. Shortcuts are disabled when typing in text fields.

profile.nextTaskSuggestionDescription: Automatically suggest the next best task to work on when you have nothing in progress, prioritizing due today tasks, then suggested tasks from your Today page.
profile.nextActionPrompt: What's the very next physical action for this project?
profile.nextActionPlaceholder: e.g., Call client to schedule meeting, Research competitors online, Create project folder...
profile.nextActionHint: Think of the smallest, most concrete step you can take right now to move this project forward.

mcp.title: MCP Integration
mcp.description: Connect tududi to any MCP-compatible application using the Model Context Protocol. This allows AI assistants to interact with your tasks, projects, and notes - works with both local and remote tududi servers.

step1.title: 1. Generate API Token
step1.description: You need an API token to authenticate the MCP server. Go to the API Keys tab to generate one.

step2.title: 2. Configure Your MCP Client
step2.description: Add this configuration to your MCP client. Replace YOUR_API_TOKEN_HERE with the token you generated in step 1. This uses mcp-remote to connect to your tududi server over HTTP. Example shown for Claude Desktop:

step3.title: 3. Available Operations
step3.description: Once configured, your MCP client will have access to these 16 operations:

step4.title: 4. Start Using
step4.description: After adding the configuration, restart your MCP client. You can then use natural language to interact with tududi:

mcp.otherClientsNote: Other MCP clients may use different configuration methods - check your client's documentation.
mcp.remoteAccessExplanation: This configuration uses mcp-remote to connect via HTTP. This means you can:

examples.createTask: "Create a task to review the MCP integration"
examples.projectsInProgress: "What projects do I have in progress?"
examples.addReminder: "Add a reminder to my inbox to check emails"

troubleshooting.title: 💡 Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.intro: If your MCP client can't connect, check that:

troubleshooting.serverRunning: The tududi server is running and accessible
troubleshooting.clientRestarted: You've restarted your MCP client completely
troubleshooting.firewall: For remote servers, check your firewall settings

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.emptyDescription: Capture thoughts and ideas quickly using the ⚡ icon in the top navbar or the + button in the sidebar
inbox.description: Inbox is where all uncategorized tasks are located. Tasks that have not been assigned to a project or don't have a due date will appear here. This is your 'brain dump' area where you can quickly note down tasks and organize them later.
inbox.unprocessedItems: You have {{count}} item(s) in your inbox
inbox.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this item from your inbox? This action cannot be undone.

project.withName: You are currently viewing all tasks associated with the "{{projectName}}" project. You can organize tasks within this project, set their priority, and track their completion. Use this space to focus on the tasks that belong specifically to this project.
project.noName: You are viewing tasks for a specific project. Use this space to manage and track tasks associated with this project.

taskViews.today: These are the tasks that are due today or tasks you've scheduled for immediate attention. Use this view to focus on what needs to be completed today. Mark tasks as completed, update their status, or adjust their due dates if needed.
taskViews.inbox: The inbox is where all uncategorized tasks live. Tasks that haven't been assigned to a project or given a due date will show up here. This is your "brain dump" area where you can quickly jot down tasks and organize them later.
taskViews.next: This view shows all the tasks that are actionable in the near future. These tasks are ready to be worked on next and don't have long-term deadlines. It's a good place to focus when you're looking to make quick progress on tasks.
taskViews.upcoming: This view highlights tasks that are scheduled for the upcoming week. It helps you prepare and stay ahead of deadlines by giving you an overview of the work you need to tackle in the near future. Tasks with due dates within the next 7 days will appear here.
taskViews.someday: The "Someday" view is for tasks that aren't urgent and don't have a specific due date. These are tasks you may want to get to at some point, but they aren't a priority right now. Use this section to keep track of ideas or long-term goals.
taskViews.completed: Here you can see all the tasks you've completed. It's a great way to review your accomplishments and reflect on the work you've finished. You can also find tasks that may need to be unarchived or referenced in the future.
taskViews.allTasks: You are viewing all tasks. This includes tasks from different projects, tasks without specific due dates, and tasks with varying levels of priority. Use this view for an overall look at everything on your to-do list.

suggestions.short: Try to be more specific about what needs to be done
suggestions.noVerb: What specific action do you need to take? Try starting with a verb.
suggestions.vague: Try starting with an action verb like "Call", "Write", "Schedule", or "Research"

telegram.welcomeMessage:
🎉 Welcome to tududi!

Your personal task management bot is now connected and ready to help!

📝 Simply send me any message and I'll add it to your tududi inbox as an item.

✨ Commands:
• /help - Show help information
• /start - Show welcome message
• Just type any text - Add it as an inbox item

Let's get organized! 🚀
telegram.helpMessage:
📋 tududi Bot Help

Send me any text message and I'll add it to your tududi inbox as an inbox item.

Commands:
/start - Welcome message
/help - Show this help message

Just type your item and I'll take care of the rest!

about.title: About
about.description: Self-hosted task management with hierarchical organization, multi-language support, and Telegram integration. Built with love for productivity enthusiasts.
about.appreciation: Thank you for using tududi! Your support helps keep this project alive and growing. If you find it useful, consider supporting the development.
